commit.message:
Disable Next.js fetch data cache for API calls

Next.js App Router caches fetch() responses by default, causing stale
data even after Redis cache invalidation. Add cache: "no-store" to
always fetch fresh data from the Go API.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Parakeet STT, word-skip fix, sleep robustness, agent routing

STT:
- Replace faster-whisper with nvidia/parakeet-ctc-1.1b via HuggingFace
  transformers (NeMo toolkit unbuildable on macOS ARM due to youtokentome)
- Add librosa dependency (required by ParakeetFeatureExtractor)
- Filter <unk> token as empty transcription
- Run on MPS (Apple Silicon Metal)

Word-skipping fix:
- capture.py prints READY to stderr when mic stream opens
- pipeline.rs waits for READY signal via oneshot channel before playing
  confirm chime — guarantees mic is live when user speaks

Sleep / headless robustness:
- run_all.sh starts caffeinate -dims to prevent display + system sleep
- keypress_listener.py: watchdog thread re-enables CGEventTap every 5s
  (macOS silently disables taps on display sleep)

AnythingLLM agent routing:
- Regular chat mode for most queries (preserves Wisdom Tree persona)
- @agent mode only for tool-needing keywords (shopping, hot lunch, YouTube)
- Capability queries ("what skills do you have") route to agent with a
  clean prompt so live skill list is returned in plain English
- New thread created on each intro event (session reset)
- Inject current date/time into every LLM message

commit.message:
Add scroll animations, nav scroll spy, body scroll lock, and expanded JSON-LD areaServed

- Scroll-triggered fade-in animations via IntersectionObserver (.animate-on-scroll)
- Active nav scroll spy using getBoundingClientRect for reliable position tracking
- iOS Safari-compatible body scroll lock (position:fixed pattern) when mobile menu is open
- prefers-reduced-motion support to disable animations for accessibility
- Favicon and apple-touch-icon link tags added
- Extended OG image meta tags (width, height, alt)
- JSON-LD areaServed expanded from GeoCircle-only to include 12 cities, 6 counties, and state
- Fix scoping bug: header moved to top-level scope so scroll spy and animations both work
- Add CLAUDE.md project context file and TODO.md task tracker

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix tree traversal by removing empty children filter

The root cause of both bugs was the !empty($children) check in
build_hierarchical_index. This check filtered out children arrays
that were truthy but appeared empty to PHP's empty() function.

Bug 1: add_element fails on empty containers
- Already had correct recursion logic
- Would find and modify parents correctly
- Issue was in INDEX not showing the added children

Bug 2: get_elementor_index doesn't show children
- build_hierarchical_index would recurse and build children array
- But then `if (!empty($children))` would filter it out
- Removed this check - now ALWAYS adds children key if elements array exists

Root cause: The !empty() check was too aggressive and filtered out
valid children arrays. By always including the children key when the
element has an elements array, we ensure the full tree is displayed.

Example that now works:
- Container has elements: [{id: "child1", ...}, {id: "child2", ...}]
- build_hierarchical_index recurses into elements
- Before: !empty check might filter out, children key missing
- After: children key ALWAYS added, full tree visible ✅

Changes:
- Removed `if (!empty($children))` guard in build_hierarchical_index
- Now assigns children directly: `$node['children'] = $this->build...`
- Simplified add_element_to_parent recursion logic
- Added comment explaining the fix

Version: 1.0.8

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: distributed agents now support HTTP/2 and QUIC scenarios

Agent was hardcoded to use FuzzerClient/FuzzerServer (TLS-only) and
only looked up scenarios from the TLS registry. H2/QUIC scenario names
resolved to "Unknown scenario" and were silently rejected.

Switch to UnifiedClient/UnifiedServer which auto-detect protocol per
scenario, add H2/QUIC scenario registry lookups, and start the
appropriate protocol server (startH2/startQuic) based on config.

Add hybrid search with BM25 and query classification (Phase 2)

- Implement QueryType classification to route queries:

  - topic_expert: Vector + BM25 search

  - general_info: Institute docs priority
  - research_area: Broad search
- Add BM25Index class for keyword matching
- Implement hybrid_search() combining vector, BM25, and structured lookups
- Add USE_HYBRID_SEARCH feature flag (default: true)
- Update /health endpoint to show search status
- Update /refresh-cache to rebuild BM25 index
- Add fallback to legacy_search() if hybrid fails
- Update README with Phase 2 documentation

Test results: 32/32 passed (100%)

commit.message:
Add two-pass refinement and court_split fallback to cross-rally player matching

Pass 2 re-scores all rallies using final accumulated profiles from Pass 1,
fixing early-rally cold start where weak profiles led to wrong assignments.
Court split now tries team_assignments before median-Y split when calibration
fails. Accuracy: 61.9% → 67.6% (+5.7pp), cross-team: 72.7% → 73.9%.

the task absolutely requires it. Dependency changes have wide-reaching
consequences for CI, load time, and downstream compatibility.

fix: inject instructions into opencode.json for opencode & blablador (#306)

Instead of concatenating instructions.md + prompt.txt via shell
substitution (which breaks the opencode TUI in interactive mode),
inject "instructions": ["/home/dev/.terok/instructions.md"] into
opencode.json config files. This works for both headless and
interactive modes because opencode natively reads instruction files.

Also fixes blablador overwriting the entire opencode.json instead of
merging only its provider section, which would clobber user-provided
settings or the instructions entry.

Changes:
- Add _inject_opencode_instructions() to agents.py (idempotent,
  file-locked, atomic replace)
- Call it from prepare_agent_config_dir for both _opencode-config
  and _blablador-config paths when instructions are present
- Revert headless prompt concatenation hack in headless_providers.py
- Fix apply_provider_config to exclude opencode/blablador from
  prompt_extra injection (they use opencode.json instructions)
- Refactor blablador script: _build_config -> _build_blablador_update,
  add _merge_blablador_config for non-destructive config updates
- Add tests for instructions injection (idempotent, preserves existing)
- Add tests for blablador merge behavior (preserves instructions,
  other providers, permissions, non-blablador models)

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: PCAP — stop file truncation on each scenario and add H2 capture

Server-side PcapWriter was recreated per scenario with 'w' mode,
truncating the file each time so only the last scenario's packets
survived. Now use a single shared PcapWriter instance across all
scenarios for both FuzzerServer and UnifiedServer.

Also add pcap capture calls (handshake, send, recv, fin, rst) to
HTTP/2 client scenarios in UnifiedClient, which previously had no
pcap support at all.

Improve schedule system UX and fix Firestore sync race conditions

Add mutation guard to SchedulesNotifier to prevent Firestore stream from
reverting optimistic updates. Enhance night track bar with WLED payload
color extraction and expanded pattern-name color matching. Wrap schedule
editor in DraggableScrollableSheet, add "All Schedules" list with bulk
clear, default new schedules to daily, and carry wledPayload/presetId
through edits. Wire autopilot off-time and payload into schedule items.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Expand pre-session warmup to full 5-minute ritual

6-phase guided experience before going live:
1. BREATHE (90s) — 5 rounds box breathing with animated expanding/
   contracting circle, count overlay, phase labels
2. VISUALIZE (45s) — Close eyes, see the apartment, Brooklyn Bridge,
   the life waiting in Dumbo
3. POWER UP (40s) — Stand, shoulders back, shake out, bounce, get blood
   flowing, feel dangerous
4. IDENTITY (50s) — Who you are, what HomeField Hub does, why they need
   you, the $200/appt no-brainer pitch
5. LOCK IN (45s) — Smile, rehearse opener, handle rejection mindset,
   volume wins speed wins you win
6. COUNTDOWN (30s) — 10-9-8... "GO. Time to change your life."

Skip button + cancel available at any time
Progress bar shows position across all 6 phases

server factory from serve.ts for testability. Add 52 new integration
and E2E tests covering keychain persistence, auto-unlock, vault
lifecycle, import roundtrips, and complete user journey flows.

commit.message:
Implement Phases 4-8: OSAL, safety messaging, verification, redundancy, timing

Phase 4 — OS Abstraction Layer (OSAL):
- 14 OSAL headers decoupling all middleware from Zephyr
- Zephyr backend in ceell/osal/zephyr/ (thread, sync, time, socket, flash, etc.)
- All existing code migrated — zero direct Zephyr includes in middleware
- CI enforcement via tools/check_osal_isolation.sh

Phase 5 — Safety-Critical Messaging:
- Priority levels (CRITICAL/NORMAL/BULK) with per-priority handler threads
- Deadline monitoring — expired messages dropped with -ETIMEDOUT
- V2 config format with per-service SLA (backward-compat with V1)
- Dispatcher + 3 handler threads at configurable OS priorities

Phase 6 — Verification Pipeline:
- CI jobs: unit-tests (twister + gcovr coverage), static-analysis (cppcheck)
- Requirements traceability (21 REQ-xxx-yyy annotations + checker script)
- FMEA for messaging subsystem
- Coverage threshold enforcement (60%)

Phase 7 — Redundancy & Fault Tolerance:
- Health monitor with HEALTHY/DEGRADED/CRITICAL/LOST peer states
- Safe state management with atomic flag and callbacks
- Software watchdog fed from lifecycle loop
- Dual-path redundant messaging with dedup ring buffer

Phase 8 — Timing & Determinism:
- Cycle-accurate timing measurement with ring buffer stats
- Timing budget tracking with violation counting
- TSN traffic class mapping stubs (IEEE 802.1Q PCP)

11 unit test suites (~130 test cases) targeting native_sim.

commit.message:
feat(tokens): replace raw total with USD cost (model-weighted)

Problem: 112M cache_read tokens per long session displays as "750M total"
but cache_reads cost 0.1× input tokens — the number is misleading.

fleet-hub.py:
- Add _PRICING table (opus/sonnet/haiku) with per-model USD rates
- _fmt_usage: compute cost_usd per event using model field from JSONL
- read_session_usage + read_fleet_tokens: store {"usage": u, "model": m}
- _avg: handle cost_usd as float (not integer division)
- _active_days: exclude cost_usd from token sum threshold

fleet-monitor.py:
- Header: show "$X.XX" today/week/avg instead of misleading raw total
- token_summary: add "$X.XX" per-instance if cost >= $0.01
- session_sum: aggregate cost_usd across all active instances

Fix crash en servidor: separar eventos client-only de ModEvents

El servidor fallaba con NoClassDefFoundError: net/minecraft/class_746
(ClientWorld) porque ModEvents importaba clases client-only y era
cargado desde el entrypoint principal del servidor.

- Creado ClientModEvents.java (@Environment CLIENT) con todo el código
  client-only: registerClientEvents(), spawnHoldingParticles(), y los
  ThreadLocals de clientTickCounter/LAST_HAND_CHECK/LAST_HOLDING_SPEAR
- ModEvents.java ahora es server-only sin ningún import de minecraft.client
- FiwstoryModClient llama a ClientModEvents.registerClientEvents()
